content.full_text:
April 13, 1992

Ms. Carla Wardlow
Fair Political Practices Commission
PO Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Ms. Wardlow:

As per our conversation this morning, I am submitting, in writing, KNBR’s request for a letter from you outlining our responsibilities in making a contribution to the San Jose Ballpark Committee’s "YES ON G" proposition on the San Jose ballot.

KNBR is the flagship station for Giants’ baseball in Northern California and as such, we traditionally post a billboard campaign every March. This year, of a total of 210 billboards, 85 were posted in the San Jose area.

After the showing went up, KNBR made the decision to lend its support to the ballpark initiative in San Jose and added a "YES ON G" snipe to those 85 boards. The snipe does not take up more than 1/8th of the total board. Please see a rough draft attached.

Although the San Jose Ballpark Committee was aware of our actions prior to the snipes being added, the decision was made by KNBR and not in concert with the committee. It is my understanding that KNBR and not the committee would need to file as an independent contributor, and that the committee itself would not need to file. It is also my understanding that our independent contribution would cover only the "YES ON G" snipes and the costs involved in printing and posting.

Would you kindly confirm this decision and send along the necessary paperwork for filing as an independent contributor. Thank you.

Cordially,

Isabelle Lemon
Manager, KNBR Promotion

The image appears to be a faxed document with some handwritten notes and a large block of text in the center. The handwritten notes on the left side read:

"Isabelle,
To Pno 195 Red or?
Please call.
Thanks, Rosanna."

The large block of text in the center is mostly illegible due to the quality and style of the fax, but it seems to contain some kind of message or advertisement. The top part of the block has some partially visible text that might say "Yes on G" and "VOTE YES". The rest of the text is not fully readable.

If you have any specific questions about the handwritten notes or the large block of text, please provide more details so I can try to help further.
